claim: Using botanical anchoring (identifying plants, then mapping their names to glyph sequences), identified approximately 14 characters and two words. Proposed a natural language encoding.
evidence_against: Proposed character values not confirmed by others. No one has extended his readings into a full decipherment. Plant identifications remain speculative. Bax died in 2017 before completing his work.

tests_run.details: Proposed character values not confirmed by others. No one has extended his readings into a full decipherment. Plant identifications remain speculative. Bax died in 2017 before completing his work.

reasoning: Historical attempt by Stephen Bax (2014). Cipher type: partial phonetic decipherment via botanical anchoring. Proposed language: unknown natural language (possibly Semitic or Asian).
source: Bax, S. 'A Proposed Partial Decoding of the Voynich Script' (2014)
